review_scope: aws:account/123456789012 — S3 and IAM permissions review

decisions.rationale: PutBucketPolicy can make a bucket publicly accessible or grant cross-account access with a single API call, regardless of whether the resource is a named bucket. The blast radius is account-wide because the policy controls who accesses all objects in the bucket. Confirmed CRITICAL even though resource is scoped.
compensating_controls: Require MFA for all sessions that assume this role
Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level
Alert on PutBucketPolicy calls via CloudTrail + EventBridge

decisions.rationale: PutBucketAcl can expose a bucket publicly or to unintended external principals. While S3 Block Public Access mitigates the public exposure risk, cross-account ACL grants remain possible. Treat as CRITICAL consistently and require compensating controls.
compensating_controls: Enforce S3 Block Public Access via AWS Organizations SCP
Pair with SCPs that deny s3:PutBucketAcl when the ACL grants external principals
CloudTrail alert on PutBucketAcl outside approved maintenance windows

decisions.rationale: DeleteObject scoped to a named bucket with S3 Versioning and MFA Delete enabled reduces the effective risk: deleted objects are soft-deleted and recoverable. CleanupLambdaRole is a time-limited automated role with no interactive access. Downgraded to LOW given these compensating controls are verified in place.
compensating_controls: Verify S3 Versioning is enabled on target bucket
Restrict role to Lambda execution only (no console/CLI assume)

decisions.rationale: Policy flagged HIGH due to ZTA standing-access rule (no time-bound condition). However, ListAllMyBuckets is read-only enumeration — it reveals bucket names but cannot access objects. AuditRole is a named, documented role with restricted trust policy. Accepted vendor MEDIUM; added monitoring requirement rather than revocation.
compensating_controls: Ensure AuditRole trust policy restricts to approved audit principals only
CloudTrail alert if ListAllMyBuckets is called outside scheduled audit windows
Consider converting to JIT access via AWS SSO for future reviews
